items.title: Johnson and Johnson (JNJ) Reports Positive Phase 1b Cancer Data
items.description: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) is one of the 10 best stocks to invest in during a recession. On February 26, 2026, Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) provided an update, reporting promising Phase 1b data for pasritamig, a pioneering bispecific T-cell engager, combined with docetaxel for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer. The study was unveiled at the 2026 […]

items.title: Retirees Take Note: DGRO Yields 2.4%, Has Raised Its Dividend Every Single Year for Over a Decade
items.description: DGRO has raised its annual dividend every single year since inception, through a pandemic, a rate-hiking cycle, and multiple bouts of market panic. For retirees building an income portfolio, that consistency matters more than a flashy headline yield. How DGRO Generates Its Income The iShares Core Dividend Growth ETF holds more than 400 dividend-paying companies ... Retirees Take Note: DGRO Yields 2.4%, Has Raised Its Dividend Every Single Year for Over a Decade

items.title: These 7 Elite Dividend Stocks Pay $114 Billion Annually, Combined, to Their Shareholders
items.author: Sean Williams
items.description: Seven major Wall Street companies collectively return over $114 billion annually to shareholders through dividends. Microsoft leads with $27.1 billion in annual payouts despite a low 0.9% yield, followed by ExxonMobil ($17.2B), JPMorgan Chase ($16.2B), Apple ($15.3B), Chevron ($14.1B), Johnson & Johnson ($12.5B), and Verizon ($11.94B). These dividend stocks have historically outperformed non-payers, with annualized returns of 9.2% versus 4.31% over 50+ years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Largest nominal dividend payer at $27.1B annually; benefits from AI-driven Azure growth (~40% growth rate) and strong legacy business cash flow from Windows and Office
Second-largest dividend payer at $17.2B; integrated operating model provides hedging; benefiting from rising crude oil prices due to Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure
Pays $16.2B in annual dividends; benefits from economic expansion and elevated interest rates from Fed's rate-hiking cycle, providing strong interest income
